about.bio: Akshath Karthik is a Healthcare Academy student, Class of 2029, working toward a career in medicine with a focus on neurology and neurosurgery. He is an active member of HOSA — Future Health Professionals and volunteers in clinical rehabilitation at Brook Rehab, pairing classroom coursework with direct exposure to patient care.
about.mission: To understand the brain and nervous system deeply enough to repair it — combining scientific rigor with the discipline of surgery to give patients back function, independence, and time.
about.careerVision: To become a neurosurgeon — building the academic and clinical foundation now through the Healthcare Academy, HOSA, and hands-on volunteering, with the long-term goal of treating patients with neurological conditions and eventually contributing to research on neural repair and recovery.

values.title: Curiosity
values.description: Approaching the nervous system — and every patient — as a problem worth understanding deeply, not just treating.

values.title: Precision
values.description: The discipline that surgery demands: careful preparation, attention to detail, and no shortcuts.

values.title: Compassion
values.description: Remembering that behind every diagnosis is a person, learned firsthand through volunteering in clinical rehabilitation.
